headlines.text: Trump says Iran war 'nearing completion' but promises to hit Tehran 'extremely hard' for 2-3 more weeks
Artemis II heads toward the moon: crew preps for tonight's critical translunar injection burn
Supreme Court justices appear skeptical of Trump's birthright citizenship executive order
Oil spikes above $107/barrel; stocks claw back after Iran-Oman Hormuz monitoring report
35 nations hold virtual summit on Strait of Hormuz security — US not invited
Gas prices hit $4.06/gallon nationally — up $1 in one month since Iran war began
Trump threatens to pull US out of 'paper tiger' NATO amid war support dispute
SpaceX confidentially files for IPO with reported valuation over $1.75 trillion
DACA recipient Maria Estrada Juarez returned to California after judge rules deportation unlawful
Ashlee Simpson wins 'The Masked Singer' Season 14 finale — America did not see that coming

breaking.text: Iran fires missiles at Israel and Gulf states on Day 34 as UAE air defenses intercept drones; Oman reportedly brokering Hormuz monitoring deal

stories.hed: Trump Promises 'Stone Age' Encore as Iran Keeps Firing Back
stories.dek: In a 19-minute primetime address, Trump declared the war 'nearing completion' while simultaneously threatening to obliterate Iran's power grid and oil sites — a combo that markets found neither reassuring nor coherent. Iran responded by launching more missiles at Israel and Gulf Arab states Thursday morning, apparently unmoved by the president's timeline.

stories.hed: 35 Countries Meet on Hormuz — Without the US — as Global Food Crisis Looms
stories.dek: British PM Keir Starmer organized a 35-nation virtual summit to discuss restoring maritime security in the Strait of Hormuz, conspicuously excluding Washington. The IRC warned that a global food shock worse than the 2022 Ukraine war fallout is imminent if the strait remains closed into April.

stories.hed: Supreme Court Looks Skeptical of Trump's Birthright Citizenship Order
stories.dek: Oral arguments at the Supreme Court saw justices from both ends of the bench raise hard questions about Trump's executive order restricting birthright citizenship. Justice Barrett flagged 'messy' real-world outcomes, while Gorsuch asked whether Native Americans would lose citizenship — a question the government struggled to answer.

stories.hed: Artemis II Is Heading to the Moon and Nobody Can Agree Whether to Care
stories.dek: NASA's Artemis II launched April 1 with four astronauts — including the first woman and first Black astronaut bound for the moon — and is now preparing for tonight's translunar injection burn. The mission is a 10-day lunar flyby, the first crewed trip to the moon's vicinity since Apollo 17 in 1972, and it launched the same night as Trump's Iran address, which is extremely on-brand for 2026.

stories.hed: Trump's Iran Speech: The Rare Address That Somehow Made Everything Worse
stories.dek: Analysts expected Trump to either end the war or announce a major escalation — instead he delivered the same four talking points he has made daily for a month, causing oil to spike 13%, stocks to tank 600 points, and Republican support for the war to crater from 76% to 61% in a single month. Even his own base is getting the receipt.

markets.context: Markets opened sharply lower after Trump's Iran speech dashed ceasefire hopes, then briefly reversed on reports Iran and Oman are drafting a Hormuz monitoring protocol — volatility remains extreme.

gas.note: Gas is now $1 more than it was a month ago — or as the White House calls it, a 'short-term increase.'

popCulture.hed: Ashlee Simpson Wins 'The Masked Singer' Season 14 — Normani, Phillip Phillips Also Unmasked in Finale
popCulture.dek: The Fox reality series wrapped its 14th season on April 1 with Ashlee Simpson taking the crown, while Normani, Phillip Phillips, and Kylie Cantrall were also unmasked in the finale. Survivor's 50th season also made history the same night by eliminating three players in a single episode, proving reality TV remains the one constant in a chaotic world.

genZ.definition: A viral TikTok trend using a clip of Jon Hamm losing himself on a dance floor from 'Your Friends and Neighbors' Season 2 (dropping April 3), where creators overlay the footage onto their own mundane wins and childhood nostalgia moments. It blew up this week as the show's marketing became inescapable on FYP.
genZ.usage: Trump addressing the nation for 19 minutes and saying nothing new is very much Jon Hamm dancing alone at the party while the whole world watches oil prices.

tldr: Trump gave a 19-minute Iran war speech that managed to spike oil prices, tank stocks, and erode his own base's support all at once — a trifecta. Meanwhile, four astronauts are heading to the moon for the first time in 54 years, which everyone would be more excited about if gas weren't $4.06 a gallon.
